steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The researchers found that great blue herons prefer deep water during breeding season, but small dark herons do not. The conclusion must follow logically: strategies that increase water depth will appeal more to one species than the other.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'attract small dark herons to the area than they are to attract great blue herons to the area': the passage directly supports this — deep-water strategies favor great blue herons (who prefer deep water) over small dark herons (who do not). ✓
  • Choice B — 'extend the average lifespan of great blue herons already in the area than they are to attract a greater number of those birds': the passage says nothing about lifespan; this compares two outcomes for the same species rather than comparing species. ✗
  • Choice C — 'attract birds that don't typically live in wetlands to the area than they are to attract great blue herons or small dark herons': non-wetland birds are never mentioned in the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — 'decrease the area's appeal to both great blue herons and small dark herons than they are to increase the appeal to both': this contradicts the finding — deep-water strategies do increase appeal to great blue herons. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
